name: Church
note: Mellow cobble point just up the beach from San Onofre. Long, forgiving S/SW walls.
board: Longboard heaven on the small clean days; shortboard when a bigger S pushes through.

name: San Onofre
note: Southern boundary of the range. Mellow cobble point, long S-swell rides.
board: Classic mellow log point, longboard almost always; only reach for a shortboard if it jumps up with size.

name: Malibu (First Point)
note: Summer SW magnet, perfect peeling right. Needs S in the water.
board: Pure longboard point, longboard unless it's big and fast, then a shortboard.

name: Upper Trestles
note: Long cobblestone right point just up from Lowers, more open wall, holds size well. Loves long-period SW; also takes WNW. Best mid tide.
board: Long performance wall, shortboard is the call; a longboard is a treat on a small, clean mid-tide day.

name: Newport (56th St)
note: South-swell magnet beachbreak. Hollow on a good S.
board: Hollow south-swell beachbreak, shortboard. Not a log spot.

name: The Wedge
note: Jacking wedge shorebreak, needs a strong, straight S/SW. Experts only.
board: Neither's ideal, it's a bodysurf/bodyboard slab. If you paddle it, shortboard on the smaller days only.

name: Hermosa Beach
note: Home break. Consistent beachbreak peaks, takes W & SW.
board: Shortboard when there's punch; longboard or groveler on the small, soft beachbreak days.

name: Sapphire St
note: Redondo beachbreak peaks south of the pier, takes W and SW, sandbar-dependent.
board: Shortboard when there's punch; longboard on the small, soft sandbar days.

name: El Porto
note: Punchy South Bay beachbreak, holds size, peaky. Best on combo swell.
board: Punchy beachbreak, shortboard or groveler; only reach for a longboard on tiny, gutless days.

name: Redondo Breakwall
note: South-swell-only point inside King Harbor, needs a solid S/SW that wraps the breakwall. Sheltered and glassy when the rest of the South Bay is blown out.
board: Shortboard when the south wraps in with size; longboard on the smaller, peeling days.

name: County Line
note: Catches both NW and SW. Open beach exposure.
board: Longboard on the small mushy days; shortboard when a bigger combo swell sharpens it up.

name: Leo Carrillo
note: North Malibu reef and point right at the county line. Catches W/NW and S combo swells; can section over the rocks.
board: Shortboard when the reef has push; longboard or groveler on the smaller, softer days.

name: Huntington (Pier)
note: Open beachbreak, very swell-exposed N+S. Pier sandbars.
board: Shortboard most days; longboard only when it's small and soft.

name: Rincon
note: Queen of the Coast. NW/W groundswell point, long racy walls. Best low-incoming.
board: Shortboard when it's overhead & racing; longboard is magic on the small, clean point mornings.

name: C Street (Ventura Pt)
note: W/NW point. Mellower than Rincon, picks up size.
board: Mellow point, longboard on small days, shortboard once it's got size and push.

name: Topanga
note: W/NW point right. Better with west in the swell.
board: Longboard on the small cruisey days; shortboard when there's overhead push.

name: Lunada Bay (PV)
note: Big-wave PV reef right. Only switches on with solid W/NW groundswell.
board: Big-wave reef, shortboard minimum, ideally a step-up. Not a log wave.

name: Haggerty's / PV Cove
note: NW/W reef points along the PV cove. Sheltered from W wind.
board: Reef walls, shortboard. Leave the longboard home.

name: Zuma
note: Wide-open Malibu beachbreak, punchy peaks, handles size, far less crowded than the points.
board: Beachbreak peaks, shortboard or groveler; longboard it on small, mellow mornings.

name: Seal Beach
note: Mellow north-OC jetty beachbreak, softer, forgiving peaks. Good fallback when Huntington closes out.
board: Soft peaks, longboard most days; shortboard when it has some push.

name: Salt Creek
note: Dana Point's performance wave, playful rights and lefts off the reef, holds a south swell with real shape.
board: Performance wall, shortboard; only longboard it on tiny days.

name: Staircase Beach
note: Cobble reef just down-coast from County Line (stairs off PCH). Combo-swell wall, mellower crowd than County Line.
board: Shortboard when it has push; longboard or groveler on the small days.

name: Newport Lower Jetties
note: Jettied beachbreak near the Santa Ana River mouth. Punchy peaks on a S/SW; the jetty sandbars hold shape.
board: Shortboard most days; groveler when it's small and soft.
